title: Rhymes And Rhythms - V
content:
Why, my heart, do we love her so?
     (Geraldine, Geraldine!)
    Why does the great sea ebb and flow?
     Why does the round world spin?
    Geraldine, Geraldine,
     Bid me my life renew,
    What is it worth unless I win,
     Love, love and you?

    Why, my heart, when we speak her name
     (Geraldine, Geraldine!),
    Throbs the word like a flinging flame?
     Why does the spring begin?
    Geraldine, Geraldine,
     Bid me indeed to be,
    Open your heart and take us in,
     Love, love and me.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Ernest Henley delivers a powerful performance in "Rhymes And Rhythms - V"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
